ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Julia Y. Trankiem, Esq., Hunton Andrews Kurth LLP, Telephone 213-532-2119, appearing for Defendant Bevmo Holdings, LLC: (CourtCall)
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BEVERAGES & MORE, INC. Petition To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings.

Defendant Beverages & More, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration of the claims asserted in plaintiff Okan Pergil's first amended complaint is granted and the action is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Beverages & More has met its burden to show by a preponderance of the evidence that Mr. Pekgil electronically signed the Arbitration Agreement by completing an onboarding process which plaintiff accessed through his personal email account shortly after an email with a link was sent to him. (See Espejo v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1062 [declaration authenticating electronic signature established existence of agreement to arbitrate]; Cal. Civ. Code 1633.9(a); Chavis Decl. 4-9, 11, Exhs. A, C, D).
Although a non-signatory, Beverages & More can compel arbitration under the Arbitration Agreement as a third-party beneficiary, because the intent of the parties was to regulate resolution of employment-related disputes and Beverages & More, a subsidiary of the signatory BevMo!, Inc., is Mr. Pekgil's employer. (See Garcia v. GMRI, Inc. (C.D. Cal. May 17, 2013) 2013 WL 10156088, at *6 [parent company of employer could enforce arbitration agreement as a third-party beneficiary].) Beverages & More need not be named in the contract to be a third-party beneficiary. (Macaulay v. Norlander (1992) 12 Cal.App.4th 1, 7.)
In addition, Mr. Pekgil is estopped from denying Beverages & More's right to arbitrate because his action is "intimately founded in and intertwined" with his employment relationship with Beverages & More, which is governed by the arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement expressly covers statutory claims. (Garcia v. Pexco, LLC (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 782, 787.)

Order is submitted

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
